latestPlannerResponse.message: Thanks—updated with your answers and ready for spec.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: On Screening Room, users view all accessible videos for a selected project and open a video to see details. The list at /screening is infinite-scrolling and sorted by Video.createdAt (desc). Each list item shows title, current version number, and an Approved (completed) badge if any VideoVersion.isApproved. Selecting a video deep-links to /screening/:videoId, opening a detail view with the latest version selected by default and playable if its transcodeStatus is ready. If a video has no versions, show sensible defaults (no player, clear message, and a placeholder for version number). Version history/switching is out of scope and handled by a separate story.

in: Screening Room video list at /screening for the selected project
Server-enforced access so only permitted videos are returned
List items show video title and current version number
Open video detail via deep link /screening/:videoId
Detail view shows latest version (current cut) and plays it if transcodeStatus=ready
Infinite scroll on the video list
Empty state when the project has no videos
Graceful handling for videos with no versions (sensible defaults)
out: Version history UI and switching between versions
Approval workflow actions (beyond displaying completion badge)
Advanced list filters, search, or alternative sort modes

assumptions: Project context for /screening is provided via a query param projectId; for /screening/:videoId the project context is derived from the video.
List sort uses Video.createdAt descending; ties break by title ascending.
Infinite scroll page size is 25 with auto-load near 80% scroll depth.
The current version number shown is the versionNumber of the VideoVersion marked isCurrent=true; if none, use the highest available versionNumber; if no versions, display “—”.

The latest version shown in detail is the most recent cut (isCurrent=true if present, else the highest versionNumber). The player renders only if that version’s transcodeStatus=ready; no fallback to earlier versions for playback.
A video is considered completed if any VideoVersion.isApproved=true, and the badge label reads “Approved”.
Clients gain access via organization/project membership; Rivvy Producers gain access via ProducerAssignment to the client organization; enforcement is server-side.
Playback uses short-lived pre-signed S3 URLs generated by the backend from StorageObject if available; TTL 5 minutes.
Empty/error-state copy follows standard portal patterns if custom copy is not provided.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a user opens /screening?projectId=X, when data loads, then only videos from Project X that the user is authorized to view are listed.
Given the list loads, then items are ordered by Video.createdAt descending (ties by title ascending).
Each list item shows the video title and a current version number per the current-cut rule; if no versions, the version field displays “—”.
Given a video has any VideoVersion.isApproved=true, then an Approved badge appears on its list item.
Given a user clicks a list item, then the app navigates to /screening/:videoId and loads that video’s detail view.
In the video detail, the latest version (per the latest-cut rule) is selected by default and is playable if its transcodeStatus=ready.
If no versions exist for the video, then the detail view shows no player, a clear 'No version available' message, and no version number.
If the video is completed per the rule, then an Approved badge is visible in the detail header.
The list supports infinite scroll: when the user nears the end, the next page loads automatically until all videos are shown.
No UI is present for viewing version history or switching versions in this story.
When a project has zero videos, an empty-state message is displayed instead of the list.
Deep links to /screening/:videoId load the detail view directly without requiring prior navigation to the list.

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for Browse project videos

increments.title: Screening Room list and detail with backend APIs, access control, and infinite scroll
increments.intent:
Implement end-to-end browsing of a project's videos and opening a video's detail using deep links, with server-side access enforcement and infinite scroll.

Backend (Rivvy Portal Services - Portal API)
- Authorization model
  - Resolve current user from session/JWT.
  - Determine if user can view a project's videos:
    - Client path: user has OrganizationMember in the project's org.
    - Producer path: user has ProducerAssignment.client_org_id matching the project's org.
  - Deny with 403 if neither path qualifies.

- Endpoint: GET /api/projects/{projectId}/videos
  - Purpose: List accessible videos in a project, sorted by createdAt desc, infinite-scroll paginated.
  - Query params: cursor (optional, base64-encoded tuple createdAt,id), limit (optional, default 25, max 50).
  - Access: Enforce org membership or producer assignment for the project's org.
  - SQL/data access outline:
    - Validate project exists and capture project.org_id.
    - Cursor paging: if cursor provided, decode to (createdAt,id) and apply WHERE (v.created_at < :cursorCreatedAt) OR (v.created_at = :cursorCreatedAt AND v.id < :cursorId).
    - SELECT v.id, v.title, v.created_at,
      - currentVersionNumber: COALESCE((SELECT vv.version_number FROM video_version vv WHERE vv.video_id=v.id AND vv.is_current=true ORDER BY vv.version_number DESC LIMIT 1), (SELECT MAX(vv2.version_number) FROM video_version vv2 WHERE vv2.video_id=v.id)) AS current_version_number,
      - isApproved: EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM video_version vv3 WHERE vv3.video_id=v.id AND vv3.is_approved=true) AS is_approved
      FROM video v WHERE v.project_id=:projectId
      ORDER BY v.created_at DESC, v.id DESC
      LIMIT :limitPlusOne
    - Build nextCursor from the last returned row's (created_at,id) when more results are available.
  - Response shape (VideoListItemDTO):
    - items: [{ id, title, createdAt, currentVersionNumber (nullable), isApproved (boolean) }]
    - nextCursor: string|null
  - Error cases: 404 if project not found or not accessible; 400 if cursor malformed.

- Endpoint: GET /api/videos/{videoId}
  - Purpose: Load video detail with latest version and a playable URL if that version is ready.
  - Access: Allow if the video's project's org is accessible to the user per the same rules.
  - Data selection rules:
    - latestVersion = the VideoVersion with is_current=true if present; else the highest version_number for the video (most recent cut).
    - playbackUrl only when latestVersion.transcode_status='ready'. No fallback to earlier versions for playback.
    - isApproved = EXISTS(VideoVersion where is_approved=true for the video).
    - currentVersionNumber computed as in list for consistency.
  - SQL/data access outline:
    - Load video v (id, project_id, title, description, created_at) and join project to get org_id for access check.
    - latest current cut: SELECT vv.* FROM video_version vv WHERE vv.video_id=:videoId AND vv.is_current=true ORDER BY vv.version_number DESC LIMIT 1; if none, SELECT vv.* WHERE vv.video_id=:videoId ORDER BY vv.version_number DESC LIMIT 1.
    - approved exists: EXISTS subquery on video_version.is_approved.
    - If latestVersion present and transcode_status='ready', load storage_object by storage_object_id and generate a pre-signed S3 URL (TTL 300s) via AWS SDK using bucket+object_key.
  - Response shape (VideoDetailDTO):
    - { id, projectId, title, description, createdAt, currentVersionNumber (nullable), isApproved (boolean), latestVersion: { id, versionNumber, transcodeStatus } | null, playbackUrl: string|null }
  - Errors: 404 if not found, 403 if unauthorized.

- Security & performance considerations
  - Ensure parameterized queries to prevent injection.
  - Add or verify indexes:
    - video: (project_id, created_at DESC, id), (project_id)
    - video_version: (video_id, is_current), (video_id, is_approved), (video_id, version_number DESC), (video_id, transcode_status, version_number DESC)
  - Do not include playbackUrl in list responses. Only provide on detail endpoint.

Frontend (Rivvy Portal UI)
- Routing
  - /screening?projectId={uuid}: Screening Room list view for a project.
  - /screening/:videoId: Video detail deep link.

- Components
  - ScreeningRoomListPage
    - Reads projectId from query string; redirects or shows error if missing.
    - Fetches GET /api/projects/{projectId}/videos with limit=25 and cursor for infinite scroll.
    - Renders list items using VideoListItem component.
    - Infinite scroll via IntersectionObserver on a sentinel; triggers next page load at ~80% viewport.
    - Empty state message shown when items array is empty after first load.
    - Error state banner if fetch fails.
  - VideoListItem
    - Displays title, 'v{currentVersionNumber}' or '—' if null.
    - Shows Approved badge if isApproved=true.
    - Click navigates to /screening/{id}.
  - VideoDetailPage
    - On mount, calls GET /api/videos/{videoId}.
    - Header shows title and Approved badge if isApproved.
    - Shows 'v{currentVersionNumber}' or '—' if null.
    - Player region:
      - If playbackUrl present and latestVersion.transcodeStatus='ready': render HTML5 video player with controls, source=playbackUrl.
      - Else if latestVersion exists but not ready: show 'Latest version is processing' message (non-playable state).
      - Else (no versions): show 'No version available' message.
    - Provides a Back to list link to /screening?projectId={projectId} using projectId from the detail response.

- UX/content
  - Badge label: 'Approved'. Style matches design system's success/complete badge.
  - Accessibility: semantic headings for titles, aria-live for infinite scroll load status, keyboard navigation for list items and player controls.

Testing
- Backend unit tests
  - Access control: ensure clients with membership and producers with assignment can fetch; others receive 403.
  - List endpoint: returns correct order, currentVersionNumber logic, isApproved flag, nextCursor behavior, and edge case with no versions.
  - Detail endpoint: latestVersion selection logic, playbackUrl only when transcode_status='ready', and correct 404/403 errors.
- Frontend unit/integration tests
  - List page renders items, badge, version label, empty state, and infinite scroll appends more items.
  - Detail page renders title, badge, version label; shows player when ready; shows correct messages for processing/no versions.
  - Navigation from list to detail and deep-link load.
- E2E tests (happy paths)
  - Visit /screening?projectId=X -> scroll to load additional pages -> open a video -> watch player loads if ready.
  - Deep link /screening/:videoId loads directly and 'Back' returns to /screening with projectId.

Observability & error handling
- Log authorization failures and malformed cursors at WARN, other server errors at ERROR with correlation IDs.
- Frontend shows non-blocking toast/banner for transient API errors, with retry for list loads.

Cross-cutting
- No database schema changes required.
- Feature flag not required; deliver behind role-based access inherent to endpoints.
- Ensure CORS, caching headers for detail playbackUrl (no-store) and list responses (no-cache or short max-age as appropriate).

answers.06a4191a-632e-48ae-a887-4f2ee0709ee7: yes, client based on their org and producers based on org assignment

messages.content: Great—let’s get started on “Browse project videos.” I have the product backlog and full architecture context (services, data entities, UI screens) available. Would you like to highlight any specific entities or diagrams (e.g., Screening Room, Video/VideoVersion, Portal API) as most relevant for this story?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here is my understanding of Browse project videos; I have 7 questions to finalize scope.

Answers to your questions:

Q: What is the precise rule for the completion badge: latestApprovedVersionId present, any VideoVersion.isApproved, or ApprovalRequest status approved?
A: any VideoVersion.isApproved

Q: Should the Video detail include a playable player of the latest version now, or only metadata until the version switching story?
A: latest version now

Q: What is the desired default sort order for the list: orderIndex, createdAt, or title, and is grouping required?
A: createdAt

Q: Do we need pagination or infinite scroll for large Projects, and what page size or load strategy should we use?
A: infinite scroll for now

Q: How should a Video with no versions yet display its current version number and detail state?
A: sensible defaults

Q: Please confirm access rules for Clients versus Rivvy Producers: org or project membership versus producer to org assignment
A: yes, client based on their org and producers based on org assignment

Q: What is the URL or route behavior for opening Video detail: in place panel vs deep link like /screening/:videoId, and should deep linking be supported?
A: deep link like /screening/:videoId
Thanks—updated with your answers and ready for spec.
Okay, let's see if we need to break this work item into parts...
This item is small enough, so let's get the software developer to review this.
